research_missions.title: Verify William Securitary Chappell's marriage record
research_missions.note: Wife identified. Remaining work is to obtain and cite the original marriage or equivalent primary record.

research_missions.title: Trace the Chappell line toward England

brick_walls.question: Did his 1839 estate name Mitchell or other heirs?
brick_walls.focus: Harris County probate, guardianship and murder records

brick_walls.question: Which descendant branch leads toward Mitchell?
Does his Monroe County branch connect to Mitchell?
Can an original record verify his reported parents?
brick_walls.focus: Census, death, marriage, probate and land records

confirmed_facts: Obadiah Chappell was killed in Harris County, Georgia.
Wiley Hunt was publicly accused of the killing.
Georgia's governor offered a $200 reward for his capture.
unresolved_questions: Where exactly did the killing occur?
Was there a trial or indictment?
Who were all of Obadiah's heirs?
Was Mitchell Jefferson Chappell connected to this family?
records_needed: Harris County Superior Court criminal docket

escape_route.title: Possible Wiley Hunt Escape Route
escape_route.warning: This route is entirely speculative. No record currently proves that the Wiley Hunt later found in Mississippi or Louisiana was the same Wiley Hunt accused of killing Obadiah Chappell.

steps.detail: Confirmed starting point: Wiley Hunt was accused and reported to have fled from justice.
Possible matching Wiley Hunt household appears west of Georgia. Identity has not been proven.
Compiled family material may place the same possible Wiley Hunt farther west. This remains unverified.

research_evidence_feed.title: Governor's reward proclamation
research_evidence_feed.subject: Obadiah Chappell homicide
research_evidence_feed.finding: Wiley Hunt was accused and a $200 reward was offered after he fled.

research_evidence_feed.title: Harris County estate search
research_evidence_feed.subject: Obadiah Chappell heirs
research_evidence_feed.finding: No complete publicly indexed heir list has yet been located.

research_evidence_feed.title: Mitchell connection
research_evidence_feed.subject: Mitchell Jefferson Chappell
research_evidence_feed.finding: No record currently proves Mitchell was Obadiah's son or heir.

research_evidence_feed.title: Wiley Hunt migration lead
research_evidence_feed.subject: Possible accused murderer
research_evidence_feed.finding: A Georgia-born Wiley Hunt later appears west of Georgia, but identity is not proven.

branch_assessments.name: Robert Chappell and Agnes Cross
branch_assessments.reason: Documented Virginia-to-Georgia family whose descendants include John, William, Abraham and Obadiah Chappell.
branch_assessments.next_action: Trace each descendant branch forward and compare probate, land, guardianship and census records against Mitchell.

branch_assessments.name: Obadiah Chappell Branch
branch_assessments.reason: Age, geography and west-Georgia movement fit the period in which Mitchell may have entered the record.
branch_assessments.next_action: Locate complete Harris County estate, guardianship and court records.

branch_assessments.name: John Chappell Branch
branch_assessments.reason: A documented Georgia branch with surviving Monroe County probate records.
branch_assessments.next_action: Reconstruct every named child and grandchild from the full will and deeds.

branch_assessments.name: William Chappell Sr. and Margaret Cooper
branch_assessments.reason: Chronology and location fit, but reconstructed children and available will summaries do not include Mitchell.
branch_assessments.next_action: Keep dormant unless the full estate packet reveals an omitted heir.

branch_assessments.name: Allen Chappell and Crosby Dawson Chappell
branch_assessments.reason: The family occupied the correct western Georgia corridor, but published family material does not identify Mitchell as a son or sibling.
branch_assessments.next_action: Reopen only if a deed, estate distribution or tax record links Mitchell.

branch_assessments.name: Edward F. Chappell Branch
branch_assessments.reason: Edward was born only a few years before Mitchell's estimated birth and could not reasonably have been Mitchell's father.
branch_assessments.next_action: Retain only as a neighboring or collateral-family research possibility.

case_files.title: The Murder of Obadiah Chappell
case_files.summary: Investigate the murder of Obadiah Chappell, identify every surviving court record, reconstruct Wiley Hunt's flight, determine whether Mitchell Jefferson Chappell connects to Obadiah's family.
known_facts: Obadiah Chappell was killed in Harris County, Georgia.
A $200 reward was offered by the Governor.
speculation: The Wiley Hunt later found west of Georgia may be the accused murderer.
The Mississippi and Louisiana Wiley Hunt records may represent the same individual.
Mitchell Jefferson Chappell may descend from Obadiah's family, but no record currently proves this.

case_files.title: Mitchell Jefferson Chappell Parentage
case_files.summary: Identify the parents of Mitchell Jefferson Chappell.
known_facts: William Secretariat Chappell is believed to be his son.
speculation: Mitchell may descend from Robert Chappell's Virginia line.
Mitchell may connect through Obadiah or John.

case_files.title: Captain John Chappell
case_files.summary: Document the claimed connection between Captain John Chappell of the Speedwell and the later Georgia Chappell family.
known_facts: Family tradition identifies Captain John Chappell as the immigrant ancestor.
speculation: The exact generational connection remains unproven.
